questions.question: Choose the option that best completes the sentence: “Hardly had the train arrived the passengers rushed to board.”
questions.explanation: Hardly had is followed by when.

questions.question: Identify the synonym of the word ‘abstain’ as used in the sentence: “Doctors advise people to abstain from smoking.”
questions.explanation: Abstain means avoid or refrain from doing something.

questions.question: Find the correctly punctuated sentence:
options: My sister who lives in Mumbai is coming to visit.
My sister, who lives in Mumbai, is coming to visit.
My sister who lives in Mumbai, is coming to visit.
My sister, who lives in Mumbai is coming to visit.
questions.explanation: The non-defining clause is correctly set off with commas.

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: (P) He started to explore the forest nearby. (Q) Rahul was fond of adventures. (R) One day, he found a strange cave. (S) He lived on the edge of a large town.
questions.explanation: The coherent order is: Rahul was fond of adventures. He lived on the edge of a large town. He started to explore the forest nearby. One day, he found a strange cave.

questions.question: Select the word closest in meaning to ‘Benevolent’:
Select the word closest in meaning to ‘Benevolent’:
Complete the sentence: “The proposal was accepted reluctance.”
questions.explanation: With reluctance is the correct phrase.

questions.question: Choose the correct meaning of the idiom: “To turn over a new leaf”:
questions.explanation: To turn over a new leaf means to change for the better.

questions.question: Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: (P) They can learn a lot from their mistakes. (Q) Students should not be afraid of making mistakes. (R) Mistakes are often the best teachers. (S) Accepting errors positively is a sign of growth.
questions.explanation: The coherent order is: students should not be afraid of making mistakes. Mistakes are often the best teachers. Accepting errors positively is a sign of growth. They can learn a lot from their mistakes.

questions.question: Select the word closest in meaning to ‘Impeccable’:
Select the word closest in meaning to ‘Candid’:
Select the word most nearly opposite in meaning to ‘Obscure’:
questions.explanation: The opposite of obscure is clear.

questions.question: Complete the sentence: “Her approach to solving problems impressed her colleagues.”
questions.explanation: Methodical best describes an impressive, organized approach to solving problems.

questions.question: Complete the sentence: “The team faced the challenge confidence, leading to their success.”
questions.explanation: With confidence is the correct phrase.

questions.question: Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: (P) She began practicing yoga to improve her health. (Q) Over time, she became a certified yoga instructor. (R) Anjali was inspired by her friend’s fitness journey. (S) Her dedication led to significant physical and mental benefits.
questions.explanation: The coherent order is: Anjali was inspired by her friend’s fitness journey. She began practicing yoga to improve her health. Her dedication led to significant benefits. Over time, she became a certified yoga instructor.

questions.question: Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: (P) It provides access to vast amounts of information instantly. (Q) The rise of the internet has transformed education. (R) Students can now learn from online courses and resources. (S) However, it requires responsible use to avoid distractions.
questions.explanation: The coherent order is: the rise of the internet has transformed education. It provides access to information instantly. Students can now learn from online courses and resources. However, it requires responsible use.
